goal: Produce a multichannel EXR with beauty/diffuse/specular/normals/depth/cryptomatte (or object-id fallback) for Nuke compositor.
thoughts: Be honest about what RTX emits natively vs what needs a fallback. Cryptomatte in particular has been a pain point -- if not native, use object-ID pass derived from semantic labels and let the compositor remap. Denoiser on beauty, off on utility passes or Nuke's reprojection breaks. Do not invent AOV names.
code:
aov_inventory = lookup_knowledge(query="Isaac Sim 5.x RTX renderer AOV list cryptomatte motion vector")
set_render_mode(mode="RTXInteractivePathTraced")
# Tag prims so object_id AOV is meaningful if cryptomatte is absent
for prim, label in [("/World/Hero/Body", "hero_body"), ("/World/Floor", "floor")]:
    set_semantic_label(prim_path=prim, label_class="segmentation", label=label)
set_render_config(
    aovs=["beauty", "diffuse", "specular", "normal", "depth", "object_id"],
    denoiser="optix",
    denoise_aovs=False,
    samples_per_pixel="<SPP>",
    output_format="exr_multilayer"
)
capture_viewport(
    path="<OUT_EXR>",
    camera_path="/World/Cameras/Hero_Beauty",
    width=3840, height=2160,
    file_format="exr_multilayer"
)
failure_modes: Fabricating a cryptomatte AOV name that RTX does not emit
Denoising utility passes so Nuke reprojection falls apart
Promising motion-vector AOV without checking the real inventory
